contents: n raw person-days, n after vent-day filter, n after zero-vent-day exclusion, n complete-case after NEE drop; package versions
role_in_pooled_analysis: For coordinating center to verify cohort and data quality
interpretation: File 1 and File 2 n should agree after zero-vent-day exclusion. Differences from raw indicate covariate missingness.

contents: Per-variable missingness % flagged ok / low / moderate / HIGH; separate sections for File 1 and File 2
role_in_pooled_analysis: CC documents which sites have which missingness patterns
interpretation: Variables flagged HIGH at multiple sites signal a harmonization issue; NEE_prior expected absent, confirm 0% present.

dataset_cohort_scope: df_pp_raw -> df_pp -> df_dt; df_hosp_raw -> df_hosp -> df_a4 / df_a5
contents: N at each exclusion step for both files: raw load, vent-day filter, zero-vent-day exclusion, death join, complete-case
interpretation: Each step should reduce n minimally; large drops at complete-case step indicate covariate missingness.

contents: Site ID; n person-days; n hospitalizations; n hospitals; single_hospital_site flag; covariate-availability flags
role_in_pooled_analysis: Used by CC to standardize pooled inputs
interpretation: single_hospital_site = TRUE means random intercept dropped and fixed-effects fallback used.

contents: Per-variable missingness on derived analytic variable list with expected_missing flag
role_in_pooled_analysis: CC distinguishes structural NAs from data-quality NAs
interpretation: Day 1 prior-day NAs are expected; any other variable >10% missing triggers MI sensitivity analysis.

contents: Patient + hospitalization characteristics overall and by SAT delivery group
interpretation: Differences in characteristics by delivery group reveal confounding patterns; sicker patients expected in neither group.

contents: SAT and SBT delivery rates by hospital_id; primary and sensitivity exposure definitions; n hospitals contributing
interpretation: SBT_2min rate (primary) expected higher than SBT_5min rate (sensitivity).

contents: VFD-28 median [IQR], % VFD=0, % VFD=28 overall and by delivery group; full cohort and survivor-only
interpretation: High % VFD=0 in neither group vs high % VFD=28 in both group is the expected pattern.

contents: OR, 95% CI, SE, z, p; SAT and SBT exposures highlighted; covariate ORs; convergence; n hospitals
interpretation: OR > 1 for SAT and SBT = faster extubation hazard; magnitude ~2.2+ at pilot. Concordance with Cox/Fine-Gray strengthens construct validity.

contents: RE variance (hospital intercept), SD, ICC; model type (glmer vs glm fallback); convergence warnings
role_in_pooled_analysis: CC documents heterogeneity in clustering across sites
interpretation: Near-zero variance = hospital contributes little beyond patient factors; single-hospital sites show fallback.

contents: HR, 95% CI, SE, z, p; gamma frailty variance; cluster term
role_in_pooled_analysis: CC compares HR vs OR direction across sites
interpretation: Agreement between Cox HR and discrete-time OR confirms finding is not sensitive to time discretization.

contents: sHR, 95% CI, SE, z, p; SAT_prop and SBT_prop highlighted
interpretation: sHR > 1 = expected direction; if cause-specific HR > Fine-Gray sHR, survival benefit attenuated by competing deaths.

contents: CIF data points for extubation and death by group; time-stamped
interpretation: Diverging CIF curves early = SAT/SBT effect appears quickly. Crossing curves indicate time-varying effect.

contents: OR and 95% CI from sensitivity runs (3S1: SAT_modified + SBT_2min; 3S2: SAT_primary + SBT_5min); variant label
interpretation: Estimates materially similar = robust to definition variant; large differences quantify 2min vs 5min impact.

analysis_section: Analysis 4.1 - Part 1: Mixed-Effects Logistic (Alive at 28 Days)
contents: OR, 95% CI, SE, z, p; SAT_prop and SBT_prop highlighted; covariate ORs; RE variance
interpretation: OR > 1 = protective for survival. Pilot: SAT 1.71, SBT 1.80 - strong survival signal.

analysis_section: Analysis 4.2 - Part 2: Mixed-Effects NB (VFDs Among Survivors)
contents: IRR, 95% CI, SE, z, p; overdispersion; convergence
interpretation: IRR > 1 = more VFDs among survivors. Pilot: SAT IRR 1.07; SBT IRR 1.01 (null). Survival benefit captured in Part 1.

contents: OR (Part 1) and IRR (Part 2) from sensitivity exposure variants; variant label
interpretation: Large differences indicate SBT-threshold materially affects VFD conclusions.

contents: IRR, 95% CI, SE, z, p; covariate IRRs; overdispersion
interpretation: IRR < 1 = shorter LOS = expected for SAT. SBT IRR > 1 reflects survivor bias - acknowledge in paper.

contents: Pearson chi2/df overdispersion stat; flag if > 1.5
interpretation: Stat > 1.5 across sites = structural, not site-specific.

contents: OR, 95% CI, SE, z, p; covariate ORs; RE variance
interpretation: OR < 1 = lower mortality. Pilot: SAT 0.586, SBT 0.555 - tight CIs, headline finding.

contents: IRR (LOS) and OR (mortality) from sensitivity variants
interpretation: SBT-LOS paradox expected to persist; mortality estimates stable across variants.

contents: CIF time points + estimates + SE for extubation and death by delivery group
interpretation: Diverging CIF by group is expected visual.

contents: 1st-99th percentile of VFD_28 by delivery group in 1-percentile increments
interpretation: Bimodal distribution expected; shift toward higher VFDs in both group is expected visual.

contents: 1st-99th percentile of ICU_LOS by delivery group
interpretation: Right-skewed expected; bimodal structure consistent with overdispersion.
